mission.title: Install and Configure Oras on Kubernetes
mission.description: ORAS is an OCI registry client that allows you to manage content like artifacts, images, and packages. Installing ORAS enables you to interact with OCI-compliant registries effectively.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for ORAS
steps.description:
First, create a dedicated namespace for ORAS to keep your resources organized. Run the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace oras
```

steps.title: Deploy ORAS Components
steps.description:
Next, apply the ORAS deployment manifests from the project repository. Use the following commands to deploy the necessary components:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/deployment.yaml -n oras
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/service.yaml -n oras
```

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
After deploying, check if the ORAS pods are running correctly. Use the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the ORAS pods running in the 'oras' namespace. You can verify the installation by checking the pod status and accessing the ORAS CLI through port forwarding.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/deployment.yaml -n oras
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/service.yaml -n oras

uninstall.title: Remove ORAS Deployment
uninstall.description:
To uninstall ORAS, delete the deployment and service using the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/deployment.yaml -n oras
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/service.yaml -n oras
kubectl delete namespace oras
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ORAS
upgrade.description:
To upgrade ORAS, first delete the existing deployment and service, then apply the new manifests. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/deployment.yaml -n oras
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/service.yaml -n oras
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/deployment.yaml -n oras
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oras-project/oras/main/deploy/service.yaml -n oras
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the ORAS pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n oras
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the ORAS service, ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the endpoints:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n oras
kubectl get endpoints -n oras
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource-related issues, verify that your cluster has enough resources allocated. You can check node resources with:
```bash
kubectl describe nodes
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your local machine.
